title: Ancient Animal Discovery in New Zealand Cave
summary: Scientists found old animal bones in a cave in New Zealand. These bones are about 1 million years old and include a new type of parrot related to the kākāpō, which is a big bird that cannot fly. The discovery has fossils from 12 bird species and 4 frog species, showing what New Zealand's nature was like long ago. The animals died out because of big changes in climate and volcanoes, even before people came to New Zealand. This helps scientists learn about ancient forests and animals that lived there. The team includes experts from Australia and New Zealand who studied the bones to understand this history.

background_read: New Zealand is an island country with unique animals like the kākāpō, a flightless parrot. Fossils are important for learning about ancient life. Climate change and volcanoes can affect animals by changing their homes. Scientists use fossils to understand how species lived and died long before humans arrived.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from Australia and New Zealand
WHAT: Found ancient animal fossils including a new parrot species
WHERE: In a cave near Waitomo on New Zealand's North Island
WHY: To learn about New Zealand's ancient ecosystems and extinctions

perspectives.description: Finding fossils helps us understand ancient life and environmental changes.
